channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: CNI 2.0 issues in London
item.description: Jul 17, 06:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 05:47 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is currently investigating issues related to CNI 2.0 in London. Customers utilizing CNI 2.0 through London (LHR) data center will experience a failure to send traffic over their CNI. We are...

item.title: XPENG Brand Day & Global Launch of MONA L03
item.description: XPENG launches MONA L03 globally in Munich, available in 65 countries with BEV and EREV options. Features Turing AI ADAS, smart cockpit, and dual-powertrain strategy.

item.title: AI-Native Insurance for Agentic AI: Pricing, Underwriting, and End-to-End Automation
item.description: arXiv:2607.13230v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI introduces new insurance challenges because autonomous AI systems can make decisions, invoke tools, modify external environments, and interact with third-party services. This paper develops an AI-native mathematical framework for...

item.title: Cost-Optimal Foundation Model Deployment Portfolio for Transportation Management
item.description: arXiv:2607.13239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models, including large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), are increasingly used for transportation management center (TMC) tasks such as anomaly detection, incident reporting, and traveler information. Deploying...

item.title: Harness Handbook: Making Evolving Agent Harnesses Readable,Navigable, and Editable
item.description: arXiv:2607.13285v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The capability of a modern AI agent depends not only on its foundation model but also on its harness, which constructs prompts, manages state, invokes tools, and coordinates execution. As models, APIs, environments, and requirements evolve, the...

item.title: Theory-Level Autoformalization: From Isolated Statements to Unified Formal Knowledge Bases
item.description: arXiv:2607.13292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoformalization translates informal natural language into formal, machine-verifiable languages. While most work focuses on individual statements, real formalization efforts are inherently theory-level: they require an entire web of axioms,...

item.title: EZSMT Version 3, Matured
item.description: arXiv:2607.13344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Constraint Answer Set Programming (CASP) is a hybrid reasoning paradigm that combines Answer Set Programming (ASP) with Constraint Processing and Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT), enabling powerful declarative encodings of complex combinatorial...

item.title: Position: Explainability Research Must Prioritize Foundations over Ad-hoc Methods
item.description: arXiv:2607.14123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the proliferation of Explainable AI (XAI) techniques -- from feature attributions to sparse autoencoders -- explanations rarely influence real-world workflows. In practice, they are often generated and discarded without guiding meaningful...

item.title: CARPRT: Class-Aware Zero-Shot Prompt Reweighting for Black-Box Vision-Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.14125v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) enable zero-shot image classification by computing the similarity score between an image and textual descriptions, typically formed by inserting a class label (e.g., "cat") into a prompt (e.g., "a photo of...

item.title: Explainable Geospatial AI for Satellite Ground Station Siting Using LiDAR-Derived Terrain Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2607.14127v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representative clutter height (RCH) is a key parameter in radio propagation and interference analysis because it captures the dominant height of local obstructions that drive terminal clutter loss. Current practice often relies on fixed clutter...

item.title: Certified Domain Consistency for Multi-Domain Retrieval: Label-Free Per-Domain Contamination Control with Conformal Risk Guarantees
item.description: arXiv:2607.14157v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval over corpora that mix several domains often returns relevant but wrong-domain evidence that ranking metrics miss and that conformal risk control bounds only marginally, under-covering the worst domains. This work introduces C3R, a drop-in...

item.title: QFireNet: A Quantum-Enhanced U-Net for Wildfire Segmentation from Sentinel-2 Imagery
item.description: arXiv:2607.14160v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wildfire detection from satellite imagery is a semantic image segmentation problem that has proven to be difficult due to challenges such as class imbalance, feature complexity, and atmospheric interference. In this paper, we build on the foundational...

item.title: Just Keep Prompting: Evaluating Repetitive Socratic Prompting in VLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.14099v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deploying Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in real-world settings requires not only strong visual reasoning but also stability under sustained conversational pressure. We introduce Just Keep Prompting (JKP), a multi-turn evaluation framework that...

item.title: Quantum Compositional NLP for Arabic: Grammar, Morphology, and Word Sense in Circuit Topology
item.description: arXiv:2607.14100v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first application of pregroup grammar-based quantum compositional natural language processing (QNLP) to Arabic; a morphologically rich, free-word-order language whose structural complexity provides a uniquely demanding testbed for...

item.title: LBA: Textual Hard-Label Adversarial Attack under Low Query Budgets
item.description: arXiv:2607.14101v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating high-quality adversarial texts with low query budgets remains a challenging problem in the hard-label scenario. Most existing approaches rely on greedy algorithms, where one position in the text is selected for substitution, followed by the...

item.title: UniSAGE: Unifying Static and Dynamic Attributes with Hyper-Structure
item.description: arXiv:2607.14102v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the rapid growth of digital data, real-world applications increasingly involve hierarchical information that combines static attributes with dynamic records. Modeling such heterogeneous data in a unified and generalizable manner remains...

item.title: Latent Communication Between Language Model Agents: Channels, Alignment, and the Limits of Text
item.description: arXiv:2607.14103v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) are utilized in many contexts and many professions. Those MAS rely on inter-agent communication, usually implemented by clear-text message passing. We hypothesize that Large Language Models may have a world model at their...

item.title: China Visual AI Leader SenseTime Declares End of Stitch-Monster Era With Open-Source Unified Vision Model
item.description: SenseTime open-sources SenseNova-Vision, a single model unifying detection, segmentation, depth prediction and 3D reconstruction, topping HuggingFace Any-to-Any Leaderboard globally.

item.title: BXI Robotics Raises Millions as Humanoid Robot ODM: Full-Stack Self-Developed Motors, Controls, and Algorithms
item.description: BXI Robotics secures funding from Suochen Technology after Feirongda backing, providing full-stack humanoid ODM solutions from motors to algorithms with marathon-verified reliability.

item.title: On the Eve of Edge AI Explosion, ModelBest Valuation Surpasses $2.8B After MiniCPM Success on Galaxy AI
item.description: ModelBest raises 5 billion RMB in H1 2026 as edge AI pure-play unicorn with MiniCPM models powering Samsung Galaxy AI and automotive deployments.

item.title: WAIC 2026 Top 10 Highlights: Chinese Chips Line Up in Full Force as AI Enters the Industrial Era
item.description: WAIC 2026 opens with 100+ chip companies, supernode clusters, humanoid robots, and 64 consumer AI products across three Shanghai zones.

item.title: [AINews] Kimi K3 2.8T-A50B: the largest open model ever released; Opus 4.8-class at Sonnet 5 pricing
item.description: a great week for open models continues.

item.title: Increased HTTP 500 Errors in Querétaro, Mexico
item.description: Jul 17, 01:42 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 17, 01:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 17, 01:25 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 500 errors in Querétaro, Mexico. We are working to analyse...

item.title: Elevated Errors China Network
item.description: Jul 17, 00:19 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 16, 22:43 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are currently monitoring the results. Jul 16, 22:10 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 16, 21:47 UTC...

item.title: Elevated errors for Github connector
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational)

item.title: Degraded REST API Availability
item.description: Jul 17, 00:14 UTC Resolved - From 22:21 UTC - 23:50 UTC on July 16, 2026, the REST API experienced significant degradation. During this period, about 39% of REST API requests failed with HTTP 500 level responses, with the errors peaking at 44.3%. We identified the issue as an infrastructure change...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 experiencing degraded performance
item.description: Jul 16, 22:04 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 16, 21:06 UTC Update - We are experiencing degraded availability for the Claude Fable...

item.title: Elevated errors for multiple models
item.description: Jul 16, 20:58 UTC Update - At this time, we are seeing recovery on requests to most models. We are continuing to see elevated errors on requests to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and are working to resolve these remaining issues. We will provide an additional update as soon as possible. Jul 16, 20:07...

item.title: CNI 2.0 Experiencing Intermittent 5XX Errors
item.description: Jul 16, 20:20 UTC Resolved - This incident is now resolved. Jul 16, 19:47 UTC Monitoring - We have identified and applied a fix for this issue and are currently monitoring the results. Jul 16, 19:07 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating an issue where customers may experience errors...

item.title: Durable Objects Increased Errors
item.description: Jul 16, 17:14 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jul 16, 16:53 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jul 16, 16:49 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 16, 16:44 UTC Update -...

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
item.description: Owen Flowers, 18, and Thalha Jubair, 20, were each sentenced to five and a half years at Woolwich Crown Court on Thursday, 16 July 2026, for the 2024 hack of Transport for London. The attack left 148 TfL systems inoperable and forced all 27,000 of the transport authority's employees into an office...

item.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed Ranks #1 Overall on RTEB, Advancing Agentic Retrieval
ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description: A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough. A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to someone else, and the damage moves faster than the explanation. Old bugs are back, weak defaults are earning...

item.title: Legacy Systems, Real-World Impacts: The Reality of OT Security
item.description: Legacy systems, safety concerns, and critical infrastructure risks make OT vulnerability disclosure one of cybersecurity's most challenging balancing acts. The post Legacy Systems, Real-World Impacts: The Reality of OT Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
item.description: n8n, the workflow automation platform, handed out the wrong accounts at login. On Enterprise instances configured to trust more than one external token issuer, it matched an incoming JWT to a local user on the sub claim alone and ignored iss. A valid token from issuer A carrying...

item.title: 🔬 The Lab of the Future Should Feel Like a Data Center — Andy Beam & Rafa Gómez-Bombarelli, Lila Sciences
item.description: Lila is betting that science, not the internet, is the last untapped source of training data. We went to find out what that actually looks like in a room full of robots.

item.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Sentenced to Jail in UK
item.description: Thalha Jubair and Owen Flowers were prosecuted over a 2024 cyberattack targeting Transport for London (TfL). The post Two Scattered Spider Hackers Sentenced to Jail in UK appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI Data Centers Are Being Built Faster Than They Can Be Secured
item.description: AI infrastructure introduces new security risks that traditional data center designs were never built to handle. The post AI Data Centers Are Being Built Faster Than They Can Be Secured appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have called attention to a new modular malware called TELEPUZ that's been spreading via websites infected with ClickFix lures since late April 2026. "The malware is full-featured, lightweight, and modular," Elastic Security Labs researcher Cyril François said in a...

item.title: ‘ClickLock Stealer’ Bypasses macOS Security With Social Engineering, Process Killing
item.description: The new macOS malware has targeted at least 100 users to steal their passwords and cryptocurrency. The post ‘ClickLock Stealer’ Bypasses macOS Security With Social Engineering, Process Killing appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password
item.description: ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for the password behind a fake system dialog, and when the victim cancels, installs two LaunchAgents and...

item.title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a...

item.title: Newer Models, Same Advantage
Oak Emerges From Stealth Mode With $60 Million in Funding
item.description: The startup has built an AI-powered Identity Operating System that governs all identities across an organization’s environment. The post Oak Emerges From Stealth Mode With $60 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Why heat pumps are still so hot in the US
item.description: It feels as if it should be illegal to even think about heating appliances during the height of summer—seriously, these heat waves in New York have been brutal—but we need to talk about heat pumps. The appliances use electricity for heating, they’re incredibly efficient, and they’re on the rise....

item.title: Elevated Error Rates For SSO Login
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services and seeing gradual recovery.Affected components Login (Degraded performance) Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 16, 09:30 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability. Jul 16, 09:13 UTC Investigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Our approach to bioresilience
item.description: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs are sharing our joint approach to bioresilience and AI models.

item.title: Enterprise SSO sign-in failures
item.description: Jul 16, 09:23 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Jul 16, 08:58 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.
